_notes: v6 policy scenarios. Replaces v5's continuous F23-F27 sampling for the cross-scenario (envelope) view with six discrete policy paths (3 per region). Within each scenario, F23-F26 take fixed values consistent with the policy's stated targets; only F27 (hardware doubling, exogenous) and the v6 epistemic additions F29/F30/F31 remain sampled. All aleatoric L1+L2 priors (F01-F22) are sampled inside every scenario.

ca-committed.label: CA-Committed
ca-committed.long_name: California — SB 100 + ACC II as legislated
ca-committed.rationale: California Senate Bill 100 mandates 100% renewable / zero-carbon electricity by 2045. Advanced Clean Cars II (ACC II) requires 100% ZEV new-vehicle sales by 2035. CAV target reflects current commercial / regulatory trajectory.

ca-aggressive.label: CA-Aggressive
ca-aggressive.rationale: Aggressive policy posture in which California exceeds SB 100 by accelerating clean electricity to 100% by 2040 and aggressive CAV adoption beyond legislated baseline.

ca-delayed.label: CA-Delayed
ca-delayed.rationale: Implementation friction (interconnection queues, permitting delays, ratepayer backlash) leads to slower BEV adoption and grid decarbonization than legislated targets.

oh-status-quo.label: OH-Status-Quo
oh-status-quo.long_name: Ohio — current mix, no state mandate
oh-status-quo.rationale: No state-level RPS or ZEV mandate. PJM grid mix evolves on commercial economics; BEV adoption lags national average; CAV adoption follows commercial deployment in major metros only.

oh-ira-accelerated.label: OH-IRA-Accelerated
oh-ira-accelerated.rationale: IRA tax credits for BEV purchase, clean-electricity production, and battery manufacturing accelerate Ohio adoption beyond status-quo trajectory. CAV deployment follows accelerated electrification.

oh-stalled.label: OH-Stalled
oh-stalled.rationale: IRA repeal or subsidy expiration; Ohio reverts to slower-than-status-quo electrification. Coal continues to play a meaningful share in PJM well past 2035.

exogenous_epistemic._notes: These remain SAMPLED within every scenario (F27 hardware doubling time is genuinely outside any state's policy control; F29/F30/F31 are v6 introductions).

F29.note: Multiplier on EIA AEO 2024 reference; widening sigma with year applied at sample time.

F30.note: Adds N additional years to the per-cohort efficiency-doubling delay; applied as additive lag.

F31.note: Epistemic envelope around F28's structural fleet-growth choice.

always_sampled_aleatoric._notes: L1+L2 priors are sampled inside every scenario and inside every Sobol draw. Read directly from scenarios/<region>/scenario.json data_uncertainty block.
